title: Core Space - New Expansions - Take a Look
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t3G_N5DO6us

Fresh off a board-game break for music, Daniel (@dungeondive) couldn't resist unboxing the new Battle Systems Core Space expansions — because, as ever, the game reawakens both his excitement and his frustration at not having space to keep it permanently set up. He walks through three additions. Shift Change at Mega Core is a civilian pack adding worker NPCs (with management, engineer and scientist classes) plus the scavengers Salazar and Molly, who use hidden hatches and back-passages to grab loot before the players and follow a flowchart rather than the usual d6.

The Rogue Purge pack is the standout: Purge who've broken from the hive mind, cloaked themselves in rags, and can be fielded as NPCs or as a unique crew that starts with a single Live One and crafts its own gear. Finally, the Dangerous Days campaign book collects the Mission Monday one-offs and adds harsher survival rules for a galaxy whose collapsing trade routes and hardening politics feel a little too close to home. Daniel praises the elegant, low-upkeep NPC system and Nick Greenwood's character art throughout, while warning that all the new minis are fiddly, brittle resin.

----

Core Space's NPCs act on simple charts yet feel alive and unpredictable. Which games pull off lively AI factions without drowning you in upkeep?
